channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Flying taxis could be carrying passengers in 2025, but hurdles remain
item.description: When he was still a boy making long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Why I’m making 2025 my year of dead-tree books
item.description: Over the past 17 years or so, I have happily paid for hundreds of e-books—everything from reference works to history to novels to comics. Having immediate access to all of them on a device that fits in my pocket ranks among my favorite miracles of the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: 2025 could be the year of the robot lawn mower
item.description: Skeptics scoffed in 2002 when Roomba introduced the robot vacuum to homeowners. That didn’t last very long as the little round device quickly became a fixture in millions of households.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump unveils $20 billion plan to build more US data centers
item.description: The US is set to receive at least $20 billion from DAMAC founder Hussain Sajwani to support data center infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Getting AI right in 2025: control, control, control
item.description: Businesses face challenges with AI adoption; focusing on control, data privacy, and specialized tools ensures success.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech industry sounds alarm over US AI Export Control Framework
item.description: The Biden administration’s recently introduced “Export Control Framework for Artificial Intelligence Diffusion” has sparked a heated debate in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CES 2025: Vay Expands Las Vegas EV Fleet to 100 Cars - A Disruptor in Rideshare Experience
item.description: Vay, a remotely driven vehicle service, is expanding its fleet of electric cars in Las Vegas to over 100 vehicles. The company has been operational since 2024 and has taken more than 6,000 rides. Vay's unique model offers users control while using the service, which can be cheaper than traditional ride-sharing services.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Preventing this insidious email forwarding scam that will drain your bank account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says business email compromise scams are a serious threat, as shown by Teresa W.'s near loss of thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Leading the digital charge: Inspiring innovation and fostering a culture of excellence
item.description: Organizations look at digital transformation as an opportunity to radically improve operations and increase the value of a product or service to the customer by embedding technology into the decision-making fabric and building automation into its functions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft reveals billion-dollar cloud and AI investment plan in India
item.description: Satya Nadella confirms $3 billion Microsoft investment to help equip India with artificial intelligence skills.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This Nintendo Switch 2 mockup from CES 2025 is allegedly based on the console itself, making us think an official announcement has to be imminent
item.description: Accessory maker Genki shows off Nintendo Switch 2 mockup at CES 2025 - and apparently it's very close to the real thing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 fast ways to free up space on your Windows PC
item.description: Want to free up space on your computer? With the right tools, you can quickly eliminate gigabytes of unnecessary files and get back to work — or whatever else you use your computer for. I’ve long argued you don’t need a “PC cleaner” app. You mostly just need the tools built right into Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The OnePlus 13 borrows a very useful Find My Device trick from Pixel phones
item.description: The OnePlus 13 joins the latest Pixel phones in supporting Powered Off Finding as part of Find My Device.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Transformación digital en 2025: qué está de moda y qué no
item.description: Algunos sostienen que la aparición de la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa ha hecho que la transformación digital sea cosa del pasado. Transformación de IA es el término que se utiliza para describirla.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fact-checking on Facebook was already a lost cause
item.description: On January 7, Mark Zuckebrerg made a not-so-stunning announcement. The Meta CEO said that his company is ending its third-party fact-checking program, declaring that “experts, like everyone else, have their own biases and perspectives.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Panasonic announces its 2025 TV lineup, led by a brighter flagship OLED TV with a new Prime Video Calibrated Mode
item.description: Panasonic's three new TVs include an upgraded flagship OLED, a mini-LED with richer contrast and an entry-level LED model.
item.dc:creator: james.davidson@futurenet.com (James Davidson), TechRadar

item.title: Zoox and Waymo are hoping Trump will ease barriers for self-driving cars
item.description: A group representing self-driving car companies on Tuesday called on the U.S. government to do more to speed the deployment of autonomous vehicles and remove barriers to adoption.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals
item.description: Outgoing National Cyber Director Harry Coker says ONCD could also lead more efforts governmentwide on cybersecurity planning and response. The post WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Gleam 1.7 brings faster record updates
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived with faster record updates and a dynamic coder for a custom type. Overall, the latest version of Gleam touts performance and package publishing improvements. This latest update to Gleam was announced January 5 and is accessible from GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google faces new labor board complaint over contractor union bargaining
item.description: The US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has filed a fresh complaint against Google, alleging that the company acts as the employer of certain contract workers and must negotiate with their union, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia's new GPU series led an avalanche of entertainment-related announcements at CES
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its GeForce RTX 50 Series desktop and laptop GPUs — powered by its new Blackwell artificial intelligence chip — kicking off a string of entertainment-related AI announcements and discussions at the trade show, including several terse conversations about regulation of the budding tech
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PIX 11
